configs.model: in-memory scheduler over complete DD match output

observations: DD-side complete match output can remain separate from scheduler admission.
Only selected matches are written back into the host worklist.
Action rows are produced only after an explicit scheduler barrier.
Skipped matches remain residual input for a later scheduler round.
decision: Scheduler materialization support should be designed before the first DD backend PR.
limitations: This is an in-memory control-plane model, not a Differential Dataflow operator graph.
The scenario validates scheduler semantics but does not measure throughput.
next_action: Carry selected-match admission, worklist writeback, barrier-delayed actions, and residual retention into the backend interface plan.
